head: cat_dictionary

classes.label: Content / relaxed
classes.meaning: Low-arousal positive or relaxed vocalization (a content meow, purr-like or resting sound).

classes.label: Angry
classes.meaning: High-arousal angry vocalization, often before or during a confrontation.

classes.label: Defensive
classes.meaning: Defensive vocalization: backing off a threat, hiss-like guarding.

classes.label: Fighting
classes.meaning: Active fight vocalization during a physical confrontation.

classes.label: Warning
classes.meaning: A warning vocalization telling another animal or person to keep back.

classes.label: Mating call
classes.meaning: Estrus / mating call (the long howling caterwaul).

classes.label: Mother call
classes.meaning: Maternal chirp or trill a queen uses to call her kittens.

classes.label: Hunting / prey chatter
classes.meaning: Chatter or trilling aimed at prey (the hunting 'ekekek').

dropped.Paining: Holdout F1 0.18 (unlearnable on the 124-clip open data) and a clinical label we must not assert. Pain stays a vet referral, not a translated verdict.

merged.Content: Happy + Resting merged (both low-arousal positive/relaxed; they confused with each other on the holdout, F1 jumps to 0.67 merged).

source: Openly-available CatSoundV2 union (Kaggle yagtapandeya 10/class + prior on-disk 5/class sample, deduped) = 124 clips. The full ~3000-clip CatSoundV2 DB is author-gated and not openly downloadable; this is the maximum honest open data.

honest_holdout.protocol: StratifiedGroupKFold by clip, 5-fold, pooled out-of-fold; each clip tested once as held-out. Clip-level MFCC+LogReg (class_weight=balanced). NO recording-id grouping available in CatSoundV2 filenames, so the group = the clip (documented limitation, same as dog_affect).
